metadata.split_role: Expanded red/yellow, blue-staining, red-pored, and caution-lane bolete records.

review_pass_notes.rule: Clear stale image-only review only when at least two distinct real source-image URLs exist; keep one-photo and placeholder records in review.

records.culinary_uses: Avoid for food because of bitterness.

mushroom_profile.summary: Bitter eastern Caloboletus with pale cap, yellow pores, pink-red stem tones, and blue bruising.
stem_feature: Pink to red streaks or reticulation

records.edibility_detail: Not a food mushroom in this guide.
records.mushroom_card_note: Bitter eastern Caloboletus with pale cap, yellow pores, pink-red stem tones, and blue bruising.
records.field_identification: Whitish to pale tan/brown cap, dull yellow pores that blue, pink-to-red stem streaks or reticulation, whitish flesh staining pale blue, and bitter taste.

records.culinary_uses: Not a food target in this guide; retained for identification and caution comparison.

mushroom_profile.summary: Bitter/Caloboletus-style bolete retained as a comparison species; needs image coverage and tighter local confirmation.
cap_surface: Pale to brown or reddish tones possible

records.mushroom_card_note: Bitter/Caloboletus-style bolete retained as a comparison species; needs image coverage and tighter local confirmation.
records.field_identification: Treat as a bitter red/yellow Caloboletus comparison record. Confirm bitter taste, pore bruising, stem color/reticulation, host, and microscopy before accepting the name.

records.culinary_uses: Often admired more than targeted for food here; red-pored bolete caution applies.

mushroom_profile.summary: Candy Apple Bolete: sticky bright red cap, red pores, coarsely reticulate red/yellow stem, and prompt blue bruising.

records.edibility_detail: Use only with expert-level confidence; not a beginner food mushroom.
records.field_identification: Look for a truly bright red sticky cap, red pore surface that blues, a deeply/coarsely reticulate stem, hardwood/oak association, and summer/fall timing.
records.edibility_notes: Avoid for food in this guide; keep as an identification/caution record.
records.mushroom_card_note: Candy Apple Bolete: sticky bright red cap, red pores, coarsely reticulate red/yellow stem, and prompt blue bruising.

records.culinary_uses: Do not collect for meals in this guide.

mushroom_profile.summary: Minimal bolete seed entry for Curry Bolete.
research_notes: Needs source links and fuller field notes.
stem_feature: May have fine reticulation near apex

records.non_edible_severity: Avoid — red/yellow bolete with strong instant blue bruising
records.edibility_detail: Avoid for food in this guide; keep as an identification/caution record.
records.field_identification: Red to orangish-red cap, yellow to orangish stem with reddish areas, yellow pore surface aging dingy orangish-brown, and very strong/instant dark blue bruising on handling or slicing.

use_links.label: MushroomExpert: Boletus sensibilis
use_links.notes: Instant blue bruising and comparison with bicolor-like boletes.

records.edibility_notes: Fast, strong blue bruising in a red/yellow bolete is a stop sign for this app. Keep it as a caution/look-alike record, not a meal record.

records.culinary_uses: Not a food target in this guide; retained for identification and caution comparison.

mushroom_profile.summary: Very blue-staining bolete-style record; retained as caution/comparison until source and image confidence improve.

records.mushroom_card_note: Very blue-staining bolete-style record; retained as caution/comparison until source and image confidence improve.
records.field_identification: Use as a blue-staining bolete comparison record. Confirm pore color, cap color, staining speed/intensity, host, and current taxonomy before treating as settled.

records.culinary_uses: Not a beginner food target in this guide.

mushroom_profile.summary: Dark-capped scarletina-style bolete complex; red pores and blue staining make this a caution/comparison group.

records.edibility_detail: Experienced-level ID only; keep as caution/comparison.
records.mushroom_card_note: Dark-capped scarletina-style bolete complex; red pores and blue staining make this a caution/comparison group.
records.field_identification: Dark cap with red to orange-red pores and blue bruising. Treat as a complex, not a single settled beginner species; compare with Neoboletus/Suillellus red-pored boletes.

records.culinary_uses: Not a food target in this guide; retained for identification and caution comparison.

mushroom_profile.summary: Hickory-associated red-pored bolete record; needs local image confidence before moving out of review.

records.mushroom_card_note: Hickory-associated red-pored bolete record; needs local image confidence before moving out of review.
records.field_identification: Treat as a hickory/red-pored bolete comparison record. Host association, red/orange pore surface, blue staining, and stem ornamentation are the useful checks.

records.culinary_uses: Not a food target in this guide; retained for identification and caution comparison.

mushroom_profile.summary: Small red-capped blue-staining bolete of hardwood edges/lawns; tiny red-orange dots in the stem base help separate it.
cap_surface: Brick red to dull red cap
stem_feature: Tiny red-orange dots in stem-base flesh

records.mushroom_card_note: Small red-capped blue-staining bolete of hardwood edges/lawns; tiny red-orange dots in the stem base help separate it.
records.field_identification: Small bolete with dull red to brick-red cap, yellow pores that blue, yellow/red stem, hardwood/edge habitat, and tiny red-to-carrot-orange dots in the stem-base flesh.
records.non_edible_severity: Caution / not a beginner food target
